# Exercise 6.4

Prove Lemmas 6.4 and 6.5.

**Lemma 6.4.** In the exact sequence $\mathfrak{h} \to \mathfrak{g} \to \mathfrak{g}/\mathfrak{h}$ of Lie algebras, $\mathfrak{g}$ is solvable if and only if $\mathfrak{h}$ and $\mathfrak{g}/\mathfrak{h}$ are solvable.

**Lemma 6.5.** If the ideals $\mathfrak{a}$ and $\mathfrak{b}$ of $\mathfrak{g}$ are solvable, then the ideal $\mathfrak{a} + \mathfrak{b}$ generated by $\mathfrak{a}$ and $\mathfrak{b}$ is solvable.
